name: libpod-markdown-perl
title: libpod-markdown-perl
summary: module to convert POD to the Markdown file format
description:
module to convert POD to the Markdown file format
Pod::Markdown is a Perl module that parses Plain Old Documentation (POD) and
converts it into the Markdown text format. It subclasses Pod::Parser, so it
supports all of its commands, too.
.
Markdown is a markup language to convert structured text into (X)HTML. Its
main design goal is to make the input text as readable as possible. For this,
the syntax is highly inspired by the format of plain text email. Markdown
supports features like headers, *emphasis*, code blocks, blockquotes and
links. For maximum flexibility, it also allows embedding HTML tags, both
within paragraphs (eg. <span>), and at block level (<div>, <table>).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use pod2markdown because module to convert POD to the Markdown file format.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use pod2markdown as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pod2markdown < POD_File > Markdown_File` as documented upstream for pod2markdown.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pod2markdown input.pod` as documented upstream for pod2markdown.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `pod2markdown input.pod output.mkdn` as documented upstream for pod2markdown.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair pod2markdown with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `pod2markdown --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
